message:
test(core): exercise sys_rt_sigtimedwait zero-timeout and EINVAL paths

Adds positive and negative coverage for the sys_rt_sigtimedwait syscall
veneer, which previously only had a signature assertion in the tests
module. Two new tests in frankenlibc-core/tests/syscall_veneer_test.rs:

- rt_sigtimedwait_zero_timeout_delivers_pending_signal: blocks SIGUSR1
  via sys_rt_sigprocmask, raises it to itself, then calls the veneer
  with a zero timespec. With the signal already pending and masked, the
  syscall must return immediately with signo == SIGUSR1 rather than
  blocking. Restores the prior sigmask on scope exit via a drop guard
  so a test failure can't leak a blocked signal into subsequent tests.

- rt_sigtimedwait_invalid_timeout_is_einval: passes a timespec with
  tv_nsec = 1_000_000_000 (one full second, outside the [0, 10^9)
  kernel-validated range) and asserts the veneer surfaces EINVAL
  rather than silently clamping or hanging.

Also extends both the x86_64_tests and aarch64_tests signature-pins
blocks so the fn type for sys_rt_sigtimedwait is asserted to match
`unsafe fn(*const u8, *mut u8, *const u8, usize) -> Result<i32, i32>`
on both architectures, mirroring the existing signature-lock pattern.
